mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% (CPIAUCSL > 200) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and positive equity returns over 20 trading days as investors price in future rate cuts.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in < 40% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and returns falls below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE signal tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings visibility and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS*UNRATE) fails to explain > 15% of return variance in out-of-sample tests, or the directional relationship breaks (e.g., PAYEMS up/UNRATE down with negative returns).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in RSAFS combined with rising HOUST indicates weakening consumer demand, creating headwinds for corporate profits and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined RSAFS/HOUST signal fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of cases, or the coefficient on the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals expanding industrial output, which correlates with stronger corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets price in near-term growth momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to explain > 10% of return variance in rolling 3-year windows, or 5-day returns show no significant correlation (r < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDPC1 indicates deteriorating economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reassess corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines but 60-day SPX returns remain positive in > 50% of cases, or the predictive power of GDPC1 drops below 5% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in UNRATE above 5% signals labor market deterioration, creating downside risk for equity earnings and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.02 in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in FEDFUNDS above 4.5% signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and creates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's IC falls below 0.03 in rolling 3-year tests.
